Name: Scyther
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

Name: Illumise
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It guides Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. There are scholars who research the meaning of these signs.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light on its rear to communicate with other Volbeat. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to be when the spirit of a deceased child possessed a tree stump. It now wanders the forest searching for friends.

Name: Trevenant
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Trevenant is very kind to Pokémon living in the forest. It doesn’t even care if these Pokémon take up residence in the greenery on its head.

Name: Charmander
Flavor-Text: If Charmander is healthy, the flame on the tip of its tail will burn vigorously and won’t go out even if it gets a bit wet.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your Active [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is very hotheaded by nature, so it constantly seeks opponents to battle against. Its aggression will not be quelled if it doesn’t win.

Name: Mega Charizard X ex
Effects: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 110 or less, this attack does 80 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: If you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +30 damage.
Flavor-Text: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd’s pace and direction of travel.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: Wavering flames similar to those of the sun appear on the surface of this Pokémon’s body.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They dwell in volcanic craters. It’s said that only a single pair of Magmortar will inhabit any given volcano.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: People call this kind of Tauros the Blaze Breed due to the hot air it snorts from its nostrils. Its three tails are intertwined.

Name: Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Mega Slowbro ex
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack also does 20 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Able to understand human speech and very intelligent, it loves to swim in the sea with people on its back.

Name: Piplup
Flavor-Text: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It avoids unnecessary disputes, but it will mercilessly decimate anything that threatens the safety of its colony.

Name: Phione
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Electabuzz
Flavor-Text: Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If Magmortar is on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent and instantly sends over 20,000 volts of high-voltage electricity through them.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

Name: Mega Manectric ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each point you have gotten.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

Name: Hypno
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

Name: Mew
Effects: 1 attack from among the Pokémon in your opponent's hand and deck is chosen at random, and you use the chosen attack as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

Name: Spoink
Flavor-Text: Using its tail like a spring, it keeps its heart beating by bouncing constantly. If it stops, it dies.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 cards in their hand, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes’ minds.

Name: Diglett
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.

Name: Dugtrio
Flavor-Text: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

Name: Gastly
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In total darkness, where nothing is visible, Haunter lurks, silently stalking its next victim.

Name: Mega Gengar ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Trainer cards from their hand.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Darkrai
Ability-Effect: At the end of each turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.

Name: Garbodor
Flavor-Text: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Move 2 [D] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Hunger has disrupted this Pokémon’s hormonal balance. The Electric-type energy stored in its cheek sacs has turned into Dark-type energy.

Name: Forretress
Effects: This Pokémon also does 100 damage to itself and 50 damage to all Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

Name: Mega Scizor ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Kartana
Flavor-Text: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Revavroom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active [M] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is born large to start with. It repeatedly sheds its skin as it steadily grows longer.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Take a [W] and a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is called the divine Pokémon. When its entire body brightens slightly, the weather changes.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Discard a [W] and a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can fly in spite of its big and bulky physique. It circles the globe in just 16 hours.

Name: Axew
Flavor-Text: If you see peculiar teeth marks on boulders or trees, it means an Axew is likely living nearby.

Name: Fraxure
Flavor-Text: Fraxure uses its thick tusks to neatly cut prey into two portions—one to eat now and one to save for later.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Flavor-Text: It overwhelms enemies with its prized tusks, which are sharp enough to cut through a metal transmission tower in one strike.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.

Name: Wigglytuff
Flavor-Text: The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can’t stop touching it.

Name: Miltank
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon like things neat and tidy. They are always sweeping and dusting their habitat, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Flavor-Text: The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Nasty Notice
Description: Your opponent discards cards from their hand until they have 4 cards in their hand.

Name: Maintenance
Description: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. Draw a card. If you can't shuffle in 2 cards, you can't use this card.

Name: Iris
Description: During this turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by your Haxorus, you get 1 more point.

Name: Calem
Description: Draw a card for each Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Hiking Trail
Description: At the end of each player's turn, that player draws cards until they have 3 cards in their hand.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Able to understand human speech and very intelligent, it loves to swim in the sea with people on its back.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It avoids unnecessary disputes, but it will mercilessly decimate anything that threatens the safety of its colony.

Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Manectric ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each point you have gotten.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Arboliva
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon ex that has any Energy attached. If you do, discard a random Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

Name: Mega Scizor ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Metal Core Barrier
Description: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn. The [M] Pokémon this card is attached to takes −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
